cards.title: Finding Healing Through Poetry
cards.body: Writing poetry about conflicts and challenges requires no special skills—just mindfulness and openness to what emerges. This simple practice can help parents process emotions and find healing.

cards.title: Recognizing Abusive Patterns to Break Cycles
cards.body: Children who experience abuse often repeat these patterns with their own kids and partners. Identifying abusive behavior is the crucial first step toward change and breaking generational cycles.

cards.title: Why Grandparents Lose Touch With Grandchildren
cards.body: Strong grandparent bonds grow from respect, trust, and consistent care. Small, thoughtful moments today prevent the distance that can break hearts and damage family relationships later.

cards.title: Are Eating Disorders Contagious in Teens?
cards.body: When teenagers ask if eating disorders are contagious, the answer is more nuanced than a simple no. Understanding the complicated reality offers both challenges and hope for families.

cards.title: Keeping a Deceased Partner's Memory Alive
cards.body: Reading your late wife's favorite books with your child creates a bridge between past and present. This shared experience can transform grief into connection and ease parental guilt.

cards.title: Japan's Shift Toward Shared Parenting After Divorce
cards.body: Japan's proposed amendment to its Civil Code could allow shared custody for the first time, ending sole parental rights. This change could significantly reshape how divorced parents co-parent their children.

cards.title: Parenting a Teenager With A-Level Exams
cards.body: Supporting a teenager through A-levels can feel helpless and daunting for parents. The reality is that hovering with flashcards often creates more anxiety than academic benefit.

cards.title: Family Estrangement: Understanding Complex Relationships
cards.body: Family estrangement exists on a spectrum rather than as a binary choice. Research shows mixed effects on wellbeing, with support playing a crucial role in any potential reconciliation.

cards.title: Living in the Moment With a Parent's Dementia
cards.body: Ruth Langsford discusses how her mother's dementia diagnosis changed her perspective on family relationships. She advocates for faster dementia diagnosis to help families plan and adapt sooner.
